description: Create a sync-upstream workflow and modify the nightly workflow to sync from upstream before building, ensuring the fork stays up-to-date with rustdesk/rustdesk and nightly releases include the latest upstream code.
steps: Create .github/workflows/sync-upstream.yml that: (a) runs daily on a cron schedule at 22:00 UTC (2 hours before the nightly build at 00:00), (b) can be triggered manually via workflow_dispatch, (c) uses actions/checkout@v4 to check out the fork's default branch (master), (d) adds upstream remote https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk.git, (e) fetches upstream master branch, (f) merges upstream/master into the local branch using fast-forward or merge, (g) pushes the updated branch back to origin. Use GITHUB_TOKEN with contents: write permission. Handle merge conflicts gracefully (fail the workflow with a clear error message if conflicts occur).

Modify .github/workflows/flutter-nightly.yml to: (a) add a sync-upstream job that calls the sync-upstream.yml workflow (uses: ./.github/workflows/sync-upstream.yml), (b) make the existing run-flutter-nightly-build job depend on the sync job (needs: sync-upstream), (c) keep the existing cron schedule '0 0 * * *' and workflow_dispatch trigger unchanged.
In sync-upstream.yml, ensure the workflow can be called both standalone (on schedule + workflow_dispatch) and as a reusable workflow (workflow_call) so that flutter-nightly.yml can invoke it.
acceptance_criteria: A new file .github/workflows/sync-upstream.yml exists with valid YAML syntax
sync-upstream.yml supports triggers: schedule, workflow_dispatch, and workflow_call
sync-upstream.yml fetches from https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk.git upstream and merges/rebases into the fork's master branch
flutter-nightly.yml calls sync-upstream.yml before calling flutter-build.yml
flutter-nightly.yml still passes upload-artifact: true and upload-tag: 'nightly' to flutter-build.yml
Both workflow files have valid YAML syntax
verification: Run 'python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/sync-upstream.yml'))"' to validate YAML syntax
Run 'python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/flutter-nightly.yml'))"' to validate YAML syntax
Inspect both files to confirm logical correctness of the workflow structure
findings: Both workflow files pass YAML validation. sync-upstream.yml supports all three triggers (schedule, workflow_dispatch, workflow_call). flutter-nightly.yml chains sync-upstream before flutter-build via needs dependency.
